text.title: usability-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for usability-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Helping participants who are struggling during tasks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every moment of struggle is data. Helping invalidates the observation and corrupts the participant's remaining session data.

failure-mode.description: Recruiting 5 total participants across 3 user segments (fewer than 2 per segment).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pattern detection requires 5 participants per segment, not 5 total. Cross-segment findings are meaningless without sufficient N per segment.

failure-mode.description: Reporting only qualitative observations without severity ratings or frequency counts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unrated findings give engineering no basis for prioritization. All findings appear equally important, delaying critical fixes while teams debate minor ones.

failure-mode.description: Synthesis agents trusting session notes without ground truth video timestamps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Findings reports generated without ground truth video timestamps are hard for teams to verify and may be dismissed.
